verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase beta subunit) is canonically a component of the F1 catalytic core on the inner mitochondrial membrane. However, extensive literature documents its ectopic translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane — referred to as 'ecto-ATP synthase' (eATP synthase) — in tumor cells (breast, prostate, liver, lung, AML, leukemia lines) and in normal endothelial cells and hepatocytes, where it is catalytically active in extracellular ATP production. The complex is assembled in mitochondria then delivered to the cell surface via microtubule-based vesicular transport and membrane fusion. RNA aptamers and antibodies directed against surface-exposed epitopes confirm outer-leaflet accessibility. Surface display is cell-state- and cell-type-restricted (cancer/transformed cells, endothelial cells), not a baseline ubiquitous feature.
